meaning: n.[宗](天主教西多会中的)特拉普派(的)(此派强调缄口苦修)
examples: The team initially reported three planets around the star, known as TRAPPIST-1, last May.
I've always associated Abbey beers with Trappist monks from Belgium, but wouldn't you know?
The Trappist dark ales were first brewed by monks who fled France after the Napoleonic period.
Expect to find Belgian Trappist, wheat and fruity beers, some 20 draught beers and at least six ales.
Trappist will join two other planet-hunting instruments at La Silla: known as Harps and Coralie.
